summary: Blistering Let It Grow and Terrapin Station with a tight second set
blurb: Features a scorching Let It Grow and an epic Terrapin Station > Playing In The Band > Drums > Saint Stephen sequence. Jerry’s guitar is ferocious despite a sore throat while Phil delivers thunderous bass. The second set flows flawlessly and the Passenger encore caps a high‑energy night.
review: On January 11, 1978 the Grateful Dead took the Shrine Auditorium into overdrive.  A blistering first set closed with a searing Let It Grow that set the tone for an unforgettable second half.  The band launched from Samson & Delilah straight into Sunrise before diving into a monster Terrapin Station that segued into Playing In The Band, a tight Drums/Space stretch and a soaring Saint Stephen – all described by listeners as “perfect” and “jammed out to perfection.”  Jerry Garcia ripped through his solos with ferocious intensity despite battling laryngitis, while Phil Lesh’s thunderous bass lines kept the groove solid.  Keith Godchaux sat cleanly in the mix, giving the keys a bright definition that complemented Bob Weir’s rhythmic chops and backing vocals on El Paso.  Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drove the jams with relentless energy, especially during the drum/space segment.  The night closed on a rapturous Passenger encore, leaving fans with a high‑octane snapshot of early‑’78 Dead hard‑rock momentum.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s blistering guitar, Phil’s thunderous bass, the epic Terrapin Station/Playing In The Band/Drums/Saint Stephen sequence, and the overall high‑energy flow. No recording notes consistent negative performance issues; only mixed comments relate to recording fidelity, not playing.

best_recording.reason: A solid soundboard capture with a clear, well‑balanced mix that highlights the band’s tight playing and contains no missing sections.

key_highlights: Let It Grow – high‑energy jam that launches the show.
Terrapin Station > Playing In The Band > Drums > Saint Stephen – a seamless, epic sequence praised as “perfect.”
Passenger encore – rapturous closing that caps the night’s intensity.
Overall second‑set flow is described as attractive, well‑sequenced and jam‑heavy.

band_performance.Jerry: Ferocious, blistering guitar work despite laryngitis; rapid‑fire solos drive the jams.
band_performance.Phil: Thunderous bass lines described as “thunders,” “great,” and “killing it.”
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar solid; backing vocals on El Paso and other songs praised, overall tone loved though a brief tuning slip noted in Playin'.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboards clear and well‑defined in the mix; occasional distortion on some matrix sources but generally bright and supportive.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums hammer the beat, driving the energy of jams and especially the drum/space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a solid backbone alongside Mickey, keeping tight time throughout the high‑octane performance.
